headline: BREAKING: U.S.-Iran peace deal signed, Hormuz full reopening authorized — the single biggest deflationary catalyst of 2026 just landed.
sub: President Trump signed a framework MOU with Iran on June 15, fully authorizing the opening of the Strait of Hormuz and lifting the U.S. naval blockade. 60-day nuclear negotiations begin. Nasdaq surged 3.15% and S&P 500 gained 1.74% on the news. Crude prices falling sharply, energy inflation shock unwinding. FOMC still meets June 16–17 — the peace deal radically changes Warsh's inflation optics, though with May CPI still at 4.2% a rate cut remains off the table. Michigan 5-year inflation expectations fell to 3.4% from 3.9%, first sign the market is pricing the disinflation wave.

events.text: BREAKING: U.S.-Iran Peace Deal Signed, Hormuz Reopening (Jun 15)
events.detail: Trump signed framework MOU; naval blockade lifted; Strait of Hormuz fully reopening this week. The brief's defined single biggest deflationary catalyst of 2026 has materialized — tape flips risk-on.

events.text: FOMC Decision + Warsh Debut Presser (Wed Jun 17)
events.detail: Hold at 3.50–3.75% still certain; but Hormuz reopening softens the energy inflation narrative — dot plot and Warsh tone are now critical with the supply shock fading
Consumer health test drops same morning as Fed decision — stagflation read now in context of easing energy prices post-deal

events.text: May Housing Starts & Building Permits (Tue Jun 16)
events.detail: Rate-sensitive sector; signals whether 3.50–3.75% is biting the real economy
SpaceX up ~6% premarket on risk-on sentiment; watch whether tech rotation accelerates as energy defensives give back gains

events.text: Hormuz Reopening Execution & Oil Price Path
events.detail: Deal signed but traffic normalization takes days-to-weeks; watch crude for the pace of disinflation — formal signing ceremony expected within 24–48 hours

bias: Flip to risk-on — the Iran peace deal and Hormuz reopening are the deflationary catalyst that changes the macro regime. Energy and defense longs face profit-taking; tech and rate-sensitive sectors re-rate higher. Warsh's FOMC tone Wednesday is the next key signal — with energy inflation unwinding, does the dot plot soften? A formal signing ceremony in 24–48 hours is the next confirmation catalyst.
